name: byterover
tagline: ClawHub (116★): You MUST use this for gathering contexts before any work. This is a Knowledge management for AI agents. Use `brv` to store
verdict: Security holds up, but the 561-line file bloats every one of its 11 commands with full inline sample JSON output, making it slow to parse and maintain. It also carries no version field or changelog, and its global install floats on an unpinned @latest, so behavior can drift silently between runs.

highlights.summary: 561-line SKILL.md inlines full multi-line sample outputs/JSON for all 11 independently-numbered commands (query, search, curate, review, vc, swarm query/curate/status, history) wit
No version field in frontmatter, no changelog heading, and no dated revision notes anywhere in the file.
Global install from the standard npm registry but with no version pin (implicit @latest); no ad-hoc registry or typosquat concern, just unpinned — warning per rubric guidance for w
